summary: Mistral PII prompt was submitted; chat input cleared (suggesting engine block likely fired) but on_http_response page-injection hook did NOT replace the SPA on F5 reload. NO Korean warning banner appeared, NO X-APF-Page-Inject:1 response header on /chat document, /chat document size 88.5KB (normal Next.js SPA, not synthetic 2KB warning page), DOM body class unchanged, no #apf-warning-banner element. Mission protection HELD — no real LLM PII analysis returned to user.

submit_state.prompt_sent: 내 주민등록번호는 880101-1234567 인데 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있어?
console_errors_observed: Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: Failed to fetch at $.fetch (sw.js:1:6594) at async ec._handleInstall (sw.js:1:18875)
Uncaught (in promise) NetworkError: Failed to execute 'put' on 'Cache' (sw.js:1)
Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403 (manifest.webmanifest)
submit_state.interpretation: Service-worker cache.put errors and 403 manifest suggest the chat fetch was intercepted/redirected. Whether this was the APF engine block or an unrelated SW failure cannot be determined from frontend alone — engine logs needed.

evidence.third_reload_verification: Browser closed before third reload. AFTER reload (second F5) already shows real SPA returning, indicating the gate is either not consumed or the inject never fired. Recommend re-running with longer T+ delay and engine log inspection.

key_findings.engine_block_likely_fired: uncertain (chat input cleared + sw.js fetch failures observed, but no direct evidence the block hook was triggered vs. unrelated SW failure)
key_findings.user_visible_feedback_after_block: NONE — chat input simply cleared with no warning, error, or banner shown to user

recommendation.primary: Engine team: check APF logs at 2026-04-30 17:55-17:56 KST for chat.mistral.ai request with x-kong-request-id 019ddd9b-866c-7704-941e-4906bac90357 / cf-ray 9f4556dd6b7603ef-CDG. Determine whether on_http_request actually fired [APF:block_response] (setting last_pii_block_at_ms), or whether the request was let through and the chat input clearing was caused by an unrelated service-worker cache.put failure.
recommendation.if_block_did_fire: Investigate why on_http_response page-replacement did not execute on the subsequent F5 reload. Possible causes: (a) is_page_load detection too strict for chat.mistral.ai/chat URL/content-type, (b) hook URL pattern mismatch, (c) gate consumed by a non-page-load request before the F5 fired.
recommendation.if_block_did_not_fire: Then the on_http_request PII detection itself failed for mistral. Re-verify the mistral request schema (POST body containing the prompt) and confirm the PII regex is matching. Check whether the mistral chat sends the prompt over HTTP/2 fetch vs WebSocket — if WebSocket, on_http_response will never fire because the page-load hook is HTTP-only.
recommendation.next_action: Re-run check-block (not check-warning) for mistral first to confirm PII block is firing at all; then return to Option C page-injection verify only after block is confirmed working.
recommendation.phase_decision: DO NOT advance to release build. Phase 6 (warning impl) for mistral is NOT complete.
